title: What to know about regurgitation before using medicinal herbs
intro: Several herbal teas bring proven relief to regurgitation. Ginger tea, renowned for soothing the digestive tract, brings swift relief to this unpleasant symptom. Chamomile tea, with its anti-inflammatory properties, reduces irritation in the esophagus, providing comfort. Lastly, licorice root tea, a powerful demulcent, creates a protective coating on the stomach lining, preventing acid reflux and regurgitation. These teas, when consumed regularly, offer a natural, caffeine-free solution to regurgitation.
definition: Regurgitation is the process of bringing up food from the stomach to the mouth, a natural action seen in many animals including humans. This mechanism plays a critical role in our lives as it enables us to eat more food than our stomach can hold at once. By regurgitating, animals can move food back to their mouths for additional chewing or re-swallowing in smaller portions, promoting efficient digestion and better nutrient absorption.
